clinical_presentation.history_of_present_illness: Progressive productive cough, subjective fevers, and right-sided pleuritic chest pain over the past 3 days. No hemoptysis.

physical_exam: Decreased breath sounds and crackles at the right lower lung base. No wheeze. No peripheral edema.
learning_objectives: Identify a lobar consolidation on a PA chest film
Correlate radiographic findings with vitals and exam
Formulate an appropriate outpatient vs inpatient management plan
common_mistakes: Mistaking the consolidation for a mass due to its rounded margin
Failing to correlate fever/WBC with the imaging finding
teaching_points: Air bronchograms strongly suggest an alveolar (airspace) process rather than a mass
Lobar consolidation + fever + productive cough = classic CAP presentation
followup_questions: What CURB-65 score would you assign, and does it change your admission decision?
